label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Today is her 16th birthday. So first, I need to figure out what today's date is. Then, subtract a month from that to get the answer. Wait, the last day of February is the 28th in non-leap years and the 29th in leap years. Let's check if 2001 is a leap year. Leap years are divisible by 4, but not by 100 unless also by 400. 2001 divided by 4 is 500.25, so not a whole number. So 2001 is not a leap year. Therefore, Jane was born on 2/28/2001. Today is her 16th birthday, so 16 years after 2001 is 2017. So today's date is 2/28/2017. Now, the question is asking for the date a month ago from today. So subtracting a month from 2/28/2017. But how do you subtract a month? If today is the 28th, then a month ago would be January 28th, 2017, right? Because February 28th minus one month is January 28th. Let me confirm that. Let's see, if you go from 2/28/2017 back one month, you get 1/28/2017. Because January has 31 days, so 28th is a valid date. Looking at the options, (D) is 01/28/2017. That seems correct. Let me check the other options to be sure. Option C is 01/14/2017. That would be half a month ago, not a full month. Option E is 02/03/2017, which is earlier in February, but that's not a month prior. Option A is 11/12/2016, which is way earlier. Option B is 01/21/2017. Also not a month ago. Wait, but is there any chance that the problem considers "a month ago" as 30 days? If today is 2/28/2017, subtracting 30
